title: tanacetum santolinoides
intro: Tanacetum santolinoides, also known as Santolina or False Lavender, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Tanacetum santolinoides include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Tanacetum santolinoides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to treat various health issues.
